An improved BDS‑3 PPP‑B2b positioning approach by estimating
signal in space range errors

Abstract

Simulated kinematic experiment and results
The simulated kinematic experiment was conducted with
GNSS observations from 6 stationary IGS stations. All
selected IGS stations are located in China and the surround-
ing areas, and their geographical distributions are presented
in Fig. 5. The period of this experiment was from 00:00:00
of January 10, 2021 to 23:59:30 of January 14, 2021 with a
sampling interval of 30 s. The positions were estimated in a
manner of epoch-wise, even

L1 & L2 for GPS
Elevation cut-off angle 10°
Observables weighting Elevation-dependent weighting (Ge et al. 2008)
Relativistic effect Corrected (Ashby 2003)
Solid earth tide Corrected (Petit and Luzum 2010)
Ocean tide Corrected (Petit and Luzum 2010)
Position Estimated in a manner of epoch-wise, with a priori value obtained from code-based posi-

The statistical results of the simulated kinematic experi-
ment are presented in Table 3. For PPP-B2b_new, the aver-
age horizontal positioning accuracy is 6.4, 7.0, 8.7, 7.1, 7.4
and 8.4 cm at 6 IGS stations, respectively. Compared to the
PPP-B2b_typical solution, it exhibits an improvement of
23.8%, 23.9%, 22.3%, 14.5%, 15.9% and 26.3% with an aver-
age value of 21.1%. In terms of average vertical positioning
accuracy, an improvement of 20.8%, 25.0%, 32.4%, 20.9%,
21.3% and 31.7% is achieved at 6 IGS stations, and its aver-
age value is 25.4%. It

Offshore boat‑borne experiment and results
An offshore boat-borne experiment was also conducted on
December 16, 2021, in Tangdao Bay, Qingdao, China. The
Fig. 12 Positioning errors by using PPP-B2b_new in the land vehicle-
trajectory of the boat is plotted in Fig. 13. Two GNSS receiv-
borne experimen

110 Page 12 of 14 GPS Solutions (2023) 27:110
PPP-B2b_typical is 12.0 and 10.6 cm in the horizontal and
vertical components, respectively. Compared to PPP-B2b_
typical, PPP-B2b_new shows a better positioning accuracy
with an improvement of 15.8% and 55.7%, respectively.
Conclusions
The PPP-B2b service is an important featured service of
BDS-3 free of charge and it can be applied to implement
real-time PPP for users in China and the surrounding areas.
Therefore, it has great potential in GNSS high-precision
positioning, especially where there is a lack of Internet com-
munication, such as in de
